items.headline: Nine new homes proposed for Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: The City Council is reviewing a plan to divide a vacant 1-acre lot into nine single-family homes plus seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This could add 16 residential units to the Residential Estate zone through a tentative map approval.

items.headline: Supermicro's $2.5M equipment installation at Rock Avenue
items.summary: Tech company Supermicro is investing $2.5 million in equipment installation at its 988 Rock Avenue facility. This represents significant capital investment in San José's tech infrastructure and suggests expansion or modernization of local operations.

items.headline: Summerhill redevelopment at El Camino includes landscaping overhaul
items.summary: As part of the Summerhill redevelopment project at 4345 El Camino Real, the project is replacing three deteriorating trees with 13 new trees (a net gain). This signals ongoing progress on the El Camino corridor redevelopment that residents have been tracking.

items.headline: Mountain View approves bonds for new Public Safety Building
items.summary: The City Council approved lease revenue bonds to fund a new Public Safety Building project. This represents a major capital investment in police and fire facilities for the city.

items.headline: City Council reviews draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan
items.summary: Mountain View is developing a comprehensive plan to protect local biodiversity and expand urban forest coverage. The draft plan was presented for council review on Tuesday evening.

items.headline: City planning activities and economic strategy reports presented
items.summary: The council received semi-annual status reports on citywide planning activities, major events, and the economic strategy work plan. These reports outline the city's progress on development and business initiatives that shape San José's growth.

items.headline: Sunnyvale plans 67-unit mixed-use development with retail
items.summary: The Planning Commission studied a proposed project on 4.22 acres that would add 55 townhomes and 12 apartments alongside retail space, including a new two-story building while keeping the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The development would be subdivided into 17 residential lots with commercial space representing 10% of the site's floor area.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered an ordinance revising business license tax rates in the Campbell Municipal Code. This annual update affects local businesses operating in the city.
